description: DataPrime queries for IBM Cloud Logs cost and volume analysis. Use with query_logs or submit_background_query tools.

queries.name: Log Volume by Application
queries.description: Identify top applications by log count over the last 24 hours
queries.query: source logs | groupby $l.applicationname aggregate count() as log_count | orderby log_count desc | limit 20
queries.use_case: Find which applications generate the most logs to prioritize TCO policy creation

queries.name: Log Volume by Subsystem
queries.description: Identify top subsystems by log count
queries.query: source logs | groupby $l.subsystemname aggregate count() as log_count | orderby log_count desc | limit 20
queries.use_case: Find noisy subsystems that could be dropped or archived

queries.name: Log Volume by Severity
queries.description: Distribution of logs across severity levels
queries.query: source logs | groupby $m.severity aggregate count() as log_count | orderby log_count desc
queries.use_case: Determine if debug/info logs dominate volume -- candidates for dropping or archiving

queries.name: Log Volume by Application and Severity
queries.description: Cross-reference applications with severity to find optimization targets
queries.query: source logs | groupby $l.applicationname, $m.severity aggregate count() as log_count | orderby log_count desc | limit 50
queries.use_case: Identify applications producing high volumes of low-severity logs

queries.name: Log Volume by Application and Subsystem
queries.description: Detailed breakdown of log sources for precise TCO policy targeting
queries.query: source logs | groupby $l.applicationname, $l.subsystemname aggregate count() as log_count | orderby log_count desc | limit 50
queries.use_case: Pinpoint exact application-subsystem pairs for policy rules

queries.name: Debug Log Volume Assessment
queries.description: Measure debug/verbose log volume that could be dropped
queries.query: source logs | filter $m.severity == 'debug' || $m.severity == 'verbose' | groupby $l.applicationname aggregate count() as debug_count | orderby debug_count desc | limit 20
queries.use_case: Quantify savings from dropping debug logs via type_low TCO policy

queries.name: Health Check Log Volume
queries.description: Estimate volume from health check endpoints that could be dropped
queries.query: source logs | filter $d.message ~= /health|healthcheck|health-check|readiness|liveness|ping/ | groupby $l.applicationname aggregate count() as health_count | orderby health_count desc | limit 20
queries.use_case: Quantify savings from dropping health check logs

queries.name: Hourly Ingestion Trend
queries.description: Track log ingestion volume over time to identify peaks and patterns
queries.query: source logs | groupby $m.timestamp.truncate(1h) as hour aggregate count() as log_count | orderby hour asc
queries.use_case: Identify ingestion patterns for capacity planning and burst management

queries.name: Error Ratio by Application
queries.description: Calculate the error-to-total log ratio per application
queries.query: source logs | groupby $l.applicationname aggregate count() as total, countif($m.severity == 'error' || $m.severity == 'critical') as errors | create error_ratio from errors / total * 100 | orderby total desc | limit 20
queries.use_case: Applications with low error ratios and high volume are good candidates for archiving info/debug logs

queries.name: E2M Candidate Detection
queries.description: Find high-volume log patterns that repeat with consistent structure -- ideal for E2M conversion
queries.query: source logs | filter $m.severity == 'info' | groupby $l.applicationname, $l.subsystemname aggregate count() as log_count | filter log_count > 10000 | orderby log_count desc | limit 20
queries.use_case: Identify high-volume info-level log streams that could be converted to metrics via E2M
